content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 28, 2016

Matthew Lewis
2422 Ridge Road #302
Berkeley, CA 94709

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-16-159

Dear Mr. Lewis:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the statement of economic interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Our advice is based on the facts provided. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

As a public official subject to the reporting provisions of the Act, must you report on your Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700) your interests in a trust that will not be distributed to you for another eight years?

CONCLUSION

It appears that you currently have an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal from the trust as defined in Regulation 18234(c)(2)(B). Accordingly, you have an interest in the trust for purposes of the Act and are required to report interests held by the trust, including interests in real property, sources of income, and investments, to the extent that the interests are reportable.²

FACTS

You were recently appointed to two commission positions in the City of Berkeley including planning commissioner and alternate commissioner. You are also the beneficiary of a trust established by your grandparents (deceased) known as the Matthew Ryan Lewis Minority Trust. The trust was established on August 18, 1994, slightly over two months after your birth.

_______________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Note that not all interests are reportable and a trust that contains only non-reportable interests need not be reported. (See Form 700 Instructions for Schedule A-2.)

File No. A-16-159
Page No. 2

The grantor of the trust is your grandmother, and the trustee is your mother. Per the rules of the trust, "Trustee shall be empowered in sole and absolute discretion to use principal for the benefit of Matthew...." Additionally, "The Trustee shall hold, manage, invest ... and shall distribute the income and principal as follows ... to Matthew ... upon the attainment 30 years of age ...." You are currently 22 years old and will not gain control of the trust for another eight years. You do not currently know the value or contents of the trust.

ANALYSIS

A public official who files Statements of Economic Interests is required to disclose reportable investments, business positions, interests in real property and income which may foreseeably be affected materially by any decision made or participated in by that public official by virtue of the official's position. (Section 87302.) Under the Act, reportable investments and interests include "indirect" investments and interests, which are defined to include any investment or interest owned by a trust in which the official owns directly, indirectly, or beneficially an interest of 10 percent or greater. (Section 87103.)

Further defining interests in trust, Regulation 18234(a) provides:

"An official has an economic interest in the pro rata share of the interest in real property, sources of income, and investments of a trust in which the official has a direct, indirect, or beneficial interest of 10 percent or greater.

A beneficiary has direct, indirect, or beneficiary interest in a trust if the beneficiary:

"(A) Presently receives income; or

"(B) Has an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal. For purposes of this subsection, an individual has an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal if the trust is irrevocable, unless one of the following applies:

"(i) Powers exist to consume, invade, or appoint the principal for the benefit of beneficiaries other than the official and such powers are not limited by an ascertainable standard relating to the health, education, support, or maintenance of the beneficiaries; or

"(ii) Under the terms of the trust, someone other than the official can designate the persons who shall possess or enjoy the trust property or income." (Regulation 18234(c)(2).)

Based upon the facts provided, you appear to be the sole beneficiary of the Matthew Ryan Lewis Minority Trust. Moreover, there is no indication that that the current trustee has the power to consume, invade, or appoint the principal for the benefit of any other beneficiary or could otherwise designate the persons who shall possess or enjoy the trust property or income. Accordingly, it appears that you currently have an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal under Regulation18234(c)(2)(B) and your interests in the trust must be reported under the Act.

File No. A-16-159
Page No. 3

Lastly, you state that you do not currently know the contents or value of the trusts. However, there is no indication that you could not obtain this information should you choose. If you are unable to obtain this information after reasonable efforts, you may wish to seek further advice describing the efforts made to obtain the information.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.analysis:
A public official who files Statements of Economic Interests is required to disclose 
reportable investments, business positions, interests in real property and income which may 
foreseeably be affected materially by any decision made or participated in by that public official by 
virtue of the official's position. (Section 87302.) Under the Act, reportable investments and 
interests include "indirect" investments and interests, which are defined to include any investment 
or interest owned by a trust in which the official owns directly, indirectly, or beneficially an interest 
of 10 percent or greater. (Section 87103.) 
Further defining interests in trust, Regulation 18234(a) provides: 
"An official has an economic interest in the pro rata share of the interest in 
real property, sources of income, and investments of a trust in which the official has 
a direct, indirect, or beneficial interest of l 0 percent or greater. 
A beneficiary has direct, indirect, or beneficiary interest in a trust if the beneficiary: 
"(A) Presently receives income; or 
"(B) Has an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal. For 
purposes of this subsection, an individual has an irrevocable future right to receive 
income or principal if the trust is irrevocable, unless one of the following applies: 
"(i) Powers exist to consume, invade, or appoint the principal for the benefit 
of beneficiaries other than the official and such powers are not limited by an 
ascertainable standard relating to the health, education, support, or maintenance of 
the beneficiaries; or 
"(ii) Under the terms of the trust, someone other than the official can 
designate the persons who shall possess or enjoy the trust property or income." 
(Regulation 18234( c )(2 ). ) 
Based upon the facts provided, you appear to be the sole beneficiary of the Matthew Ryan 
Lewis Minority Trust. Moreover, there is no indication that that the current trustee has the power to 
consume, invade, or appoint the principal for the benefit of any other beneficiary or could otherwise 
designate the persons who shall possess or enjoy the trust property or income. Accordingly, it 
appears that you currently have an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal under 
Regulation18234(c)(2)(8) and your interests in the trust must be reported under the Act.  
Lastly, you state that you do not currently know the contents or value of the trusts. However, 
there is no indication that you could not obtain this information should you choose. If you are 
unable to obtain this information after reasonable efforts, you may wish to seek further advice 
describing the efforts made to obtain the information.
